metadata_checks.comments: Title, DOI, year, and paper type match; the paper contains an explicit global GHG/carbon tax support analysis, so tax scope is correct.

determinant_checks.comment: Reported as a strong correlate of GCS support; more Democratic/left-leaning respondents are more supportive.
Reported as a strong correlate of GCS support; higher governmental trust aligns with greater support.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: perceptions that climate policies are effective at reducing emissions
determinant_checks.comment: Reported as a strong correlate of GCS support; perceived effectiveness is positively associated with support.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: perceptions that climate policies are in one’s self-interest
determinant_checks.comment: Reported as a strong correlate of GCS support; perceived personal benefit is positively associated with support.
US2 campaign-style exposure to pros and cons reduced stated GCS support; negative direction is supported.

final_recommendation_summary: Keep in corpus. The extraction contains only tax-specific determinants directly tied to GCS support, and all listed determinants are supported by the paper.
